title: Every release-gated HTTP endpoint must declare a latency SLO and stay within it under nominal load
statement: For every endpoint in `.devai/state/sensors/inventory_api/api-map.json#/endpoints[*]` that is not `@Public()`-marked (i.e. carries a permission binding) and is not on the bootstrap allow-list, the endpoint MUST declare a per-percentile latency SLO covering at minimum p50 and p95. The SLO is declared either (a) in a use-case `acceptance[]` line citing the endpoint, or (b) in a sibling `*.slo.json` file under `docs/performance/`, or (c) implicitly by inheriting the pack-default SLO declared in `extractor_params.perf_test.default_slo`. The pack default for stynx (redox-nestjs-postgres-angular shape) is p50 < 200ms, p95 < 800ms for read endpoints; p50 < 500ms, p95 < 2000ms for write endpoints. Endpoints whose measured p95 exceeds the declared SLO under nominal-load probe runs (every `_probes/*` exercise + `pnpm test:perf` if defined) are a release-gating finding. Endpoints with no declared SLO + no measured probe data are REVIEW (advisory, not gating) until the first promotion cycle that catches them.
rationale: Stynx surfaces dedicated `/_probes/*` endpoints (data-tx, idempotency, ratelimit, readonly-write) as a built-in performance smoke surface, but pre-Phase-30 no invariant referenced them — `sense-spec-performance-targets` (Phase 27.D) flagged stynx with FAIL on F1×T7 because the spec layer carried zero perf-typed invariants. This invariant promotes a baseline gate: endpoints inherit pack defaults if they don't author per-endpoint SLOs, so initial adopters get measured-and-graded performance for free. Adopters with stricter requirements override per-endpoint via `*.slo.json`. The 'gate' severity is conservative for an initial promotion — the actual enforcement triggers only when `sense-perf-test` produces SR readings (post-Phase-30.H ships the sensor); pre-30.H the invariant is structurally present but un-measurable, mirroring INV-RBAC-001's pre-Phase-22 stance.
